# Pagesmith

> Filesystem-first content toolkit organized as a multi-package workspace under the `@pagesmith/` npm scope.

## Summary

- Three main packages: `@pagesmith/core` for the headless content layer, `@pagesmith/site` for site-building plus re-exported content APIs and the `pagesmith-site` CLI, and `@pagesmith/docs` for the opinionated docs preset on top of the site/content stack
- Typed filesystem collections for markdown and structured data (JSON, JSONC, JSON5, YAML, TOML)
- Lazy markdown rendering with the built-in Shiki-backed code renderer, headings, and read-time metadata
- GitHub Flavored Markdown, GitHub Alerts, math (LaTeX/MathJax), smart typography, accessible emojis, and auto-linked headings
- Vite-native split: site consumers can keep `pagesmithContent`, `pagesmithSsg`, and `sharedAssetsPlugin` on `@pagesmith/site/vite`, while `@pagesmith/core/vite` remains the canonical low-level content-plugin export
- Schema validation with Zod at the boundary, plus AST-level content validators
- Built-in JSX runtime, shared CSS/runtime bundles, theme + text-size controls, and TOC highlighting from `@pagesmith/site`
- Docs defaults from `pagesmith.config.json5`, including listing pages, shell overrides, and layout overrides through `theme.layouts.*`
- Bundled Pagefind search with Cmd+K keyboard shortcut
- Assistant artifact helpers exposed from `@pagesmith/core/ai`

## Quick Start

For a docs site with defaults:

```bash
npm add @pagesmith/docs
npx pagesmith-docs init --yes --ai
```

For a custom site or framework integration:

```bash
npm add @pagesmith/site
```

```ts
import { createContentLayer, defineCollection, defineConfig, z } from '@pagesmith/site'

const posts = defineCollection({
  loader: 'markdown',
  directory: 'content/posts',
  schema: z.object({ title: z.string(), date: z.coerce.date() }),
})

const layer = createContentLayer(defineConfig({ collections: { posts } }))
const entries = await layer.getCollection('posts')
const rendered = await entries[0]?.render()
// rendered.html, rendered.headings, rendered.readTime
```

Use `@pagesmith/docs` when you want the built-in docs experience. Use `@pagesmith/site` when you need custom layouts or framework-specific rendering. Use `@pagesmith/core` directly only for headless-only integrations.

## Framework Support

Vite-based integration with any framework through three layers:
- **Virtual modules**: `pagesmithContent` from `@pagesmith/site/vite`
- **Programmatic API**: `createContentLayer` from `@pagesmith/site` (or `@pagesmith/core` for headless-only integrations)
- **SSG + assets**: `pagesmithSsg` and `sharedAssetsPlugin` from `@pagesmith/site/vite`

## Docs Content Structure

Content follows a folder convention for `@pagesmith/docs`:
- `content/README.md` — home page
- Top-level folders → navigation sections and sidebar groups
- Subfolders with `README.md` → individual pages
- `meta.json5` files → section ordering, series grouping, display names, and layout hints
- Frontmatter: title, description, navLabel, sidebarLabel, order, layout, chrome, draft (all optional)
